sections.paragraph:
ἀδελφός, οῦ, ὁ [ἀ-copul., δελφύς ‘womb’ = ‘of the same womb’]

—1. ‘brother’

—a. lit. Mt 1:2; J 1:41 al.

—b. in extended sense of affinity in common interests and activity Mt 7:3; Mk 3:35; frequently of one within a common ethnic, social, or cultic group: compatriot, of Paul as fellow Israelite Ro 9:3; neighbor Mt 5:22-24.

—2. the plural is sometimes used in the collective sense brothers and sisters

—a. lit. Lk 21:16.

—b. in extended community sense Ro 8:29; Eph 6:23.
